sources: BEA Regional (US state GDP; population derived from personal income / per-capita personal income; public domain) + World Bank WDI (country GDP, GDP PPP, population; CC BY 4.0).

nominal.label: Total GDP (nominal)
nominal.blurb: GDP at market exchange rates — the headline size.

ppp.label: Total GDP (PPP)
ppp.blurb: GDP adjusted for purchasing power (international $). US-state figures use nominal USD as a PPP proxy.

per_capita.label: GDP per capita (PPP)
per_capita.blurb: PPP GDP per person — a living-standards lens.

caveats: US-state PPP uses nominal USD as a proxy (US ≈ PPP reference economy; US PPP/nominal ≈ 1.01).
State population is derived (personal income ÷ per-capita personal income).
DC is excluded from the per-capita ranking: its GDP-per-capita is a commuter artifact (metro-wide output divided by DC residents only).
Release lags differ by source; each place carries the year of its GDP figure.

hinterland.source: OECD Functional Urban Areas (Economy) — GDP share of national value; national totals from World Bank WDI.
hinterland.note: Each country's capital and/or largest metro can be removed; values are recomputed on the remaining 'hinterland'. Coverage is OECD/EU only. Metro GDP share is applied to both nominal and PPP national totals; metro population is OECD-derived (GDP ÷ GDP-per-person).
